meaning: n.水汽,水蒸气,无实质之物vi.蒸发,自夸vt.使蒸发n.自夸者
examples: Then it snows because as the air rises, it cools off and loses its capacity to hold water vapor.
Such feedbacks might involve ice on land and sea, clouds, or water vapor, which also absorb radiant heat.
Astronomers therefore prefer dry sites with little water vapor, which is generally found at higher altitudes.
All that comes out of the tailpipe is a little trickle of water and water vapor.
The gel purportedly turns to vapor underground, then returns to the surface with the gas.
